subject: Product Management
title: Running Your First Test
description: A quick win: understand Running Your First Test.

hook.text: Step one isn't setting up the tool. It's writing the hypothesis: 'Changing X will improve Y by Z% because of reason W.'

buildup.text: Step two: calculate sample size and duration. Step three: implement the variation. Step four: launch and wait.

discovery.text: Step five: analyze results at the predetermined time.

twist.text: The most common mistake: changing the test mid-flight. If you tweak the variation during the test, you invalidate the results.

climax.text: Even a failed experiment produces value.

punchline.text: Hypothesis, sample size, launch, wait, analyze, document. Every test follows this rhythm.

quiz.question: What is the first step in running an A/B test?
options: Writing a clear hypothesis — what will change, what metric will improve, and why
